title: gentoo live ebuild for retroshare
author: bschulz81
body:
Hi, retroshare was recently removed from portage since the release uses old versions of libraries which are sometimes unsecure (botan-2) or are not in gentoo anymore.

I have written an ebuild for the github version of retroshare. It links to the supported botan-3 from gentoo and contains fixes to compile with gcc-16 and some fixes for the desktop icons. 

The ebuild can be found here: 

https://bugs.gentoo.org/977145

To install, place the string 

net-p2p/retroshare **

into a file in the folder /etc/portage/package.accept_keywords, e.g. by

> echo "net-p2p/retroshare **" >> /etc/portage/package.accept_keywords/packagelist

create a repository with a custom name like "ownebuilds" for own packages if you don't have one already  by

> eselect repository create ownebuilds
> mkdir /var/db/repos/ownebuilds/net-p2p
> mkdir /var/db/repos/ownebuilds/net-p2p/retroshare

then download ebuild file from https://bugs.gentoo.org/977145 and copy it into the directory

 /var/db/repos/ownebuilds/net-p2p/retroshare

create a manifest for the ebuild with  

> ebuild /var/db/repos/ownebuilds/net-p2p/retroshare/retroshare-9999.ebuild manifest

Then
> emerge --sync;

and finally

> emerge retroshare

should install retroshare on gentoo. 

You may use

> emerge -pv retroshare

to look at the useflags before and customize your instalation. Currently it has the following useflags:

> USE="cli gui retroshare_plugins -autologin -cmark -rs_webui -test -tor-only -wiki"

For example, to avoid compiling plugins, use
 > USE="-retroshare_plugins" emerge retroshare

The useflags are adapted to retroshare's build system. Look at the documentation for what they do in the retroshare proj files.

Since this is a live version from the github code, it won't be updated by a regular call to emerge -uUDN world.

Instead, you need to reemerge by typing 

> emerge retroshare

manually to get the program with the current code on github.

title: Retroshare/supportlibs contains a project called jni.hpp which leads to a 404 (dead link, made private or deleted project) if you click on the link
author: bschulz81
body:
If you go to 

Retroshare/supportlibs

you will find this link

https://github.com/RetroShare/jni.hpp/tree/b1f3de8ec650b1d232b284da5b231d7355d73a53

which leads, if you click on it, to a 404 unavailable...

Could you please remove this dead link? 

Portage will just mark an ebuild that describes a github project with a dead link as invalid. It wont compile a git repository that contains dead links.

I can go around that when writing an ebuild, but that would require some effort, to ask portage to selectively download and then adapt the source...

Removing the dead link is usually much easier...

Best regards,
Benjamin

title: Friend not validated yet / invalid certificate (arch Linux)
author: topas-rec
body:
I have a Windows and a Linux machine both in the same local network.
I added the Retroshare (short) IDs to each other (crossed: Windows ID added as friend in Linux and Linux ID as friend in Windows).

The Windows user is shown fine in Linux.
The Linux users identity is shown with the addition: "not validated yet".

Chatting between the two works.
I have a vague guess, that the cryptographic stuff and all regarding encryption technically works fine, just the Linux user has not been "authenticated" by the Windows user. In other words, the Windows user has not validated, that the ID or key he/she is communicating with is really the ID/key that the user wants to communicate with.
Okay.

I don't know how to trust a key/ID. Otherwise I don't know why I have to, because the Linux machine added the user by short ID without problems.

I found out that the keyring does not show the Linux user on the Windows machine. The Linux machine shows the Windows users key fine in the keyring.

Can you help me figuring out what's up here?

title: retroshare compilation depends on botan-2 which reached the end of its support in 2024 and which has security issues. Please Update retroshare to support botan-3 and block botan-2 support.
author: bschulz81
body:
Recently the release of  retroshare was removed from the Gentoo Linux distribution beccause retroshare did not compile anymore

It turns out that retroshare is shipped with, sometimes ancient, libraries.

Using outdated libraries that may not have recieved security fixes is a vulnerability, especially if you are using an application that makes use of cryptography and is advertised to be secure.

I noted that retroshare (which is a very nice project since it encrypts data as well as metadata and is decentral without needing expensive company servers) is still actively developed on github.

I also noted that some of the libraries it comes with have recent updates if one uses the master branch of retroshare on github.

So i tried to compile retroshare on the Gentoo Linux distribution by this tutorial:

https://github.com/RetroShare/RetroShare/blob/master/build_scripts/Debian+Ubuntu/Linux_InstallGuide.md

Compilation stopped with:

usr/x86_64-pc-linux-gnu/binutils-bin/2.46.0/ld: -lbotan-2 cant be found

botan-2 reached the end of its supported life cycle in 2024. 

As of 2026, multiple security vulnerabilities have been found in botan-2 which were unpatched as the package is no longer supported by the developers.

botan-2 has therefore been removed from gentoo. Users and developers are encouraged to switch to botan-3 instead.

I ask the retroshare team to change their code to support botan-3 and block the support of botan-2 in their code

A quick google search reveals the following vulnerabilities of botan-2


Recent Vulnerabilities Found in Botan 2.x (Unpatched)
According to 2025/2026 Ubuntu security notices, the following issues were identified in older Botan versions, which are only fixed in Botan 3.x:
CVE-2024-39312: Improper handling of conflicting name constraints in X.509 certificates, allowing authentication bypass.
CVE-2024-34702/03: Denial of service (DoS) vulnerabilities arising from improper input size limits in name constraints and primality checks.
OCSP Response Forgery (2026): Botan failed to verify signature validity in OCSP responses, allowing Man-in-the-Middle (MitM) attacks.
Heap Overread (2026): An invalid length MAC during SM2 decryption could cause a heap over-read. 

That is certainly not what retroshare should depend on. Failure to validate a certificate makes retroshare communication insecure.

Best regards,

Benjamin

title: WINDOWS OPERATING SYSTEM FAILURE
author: All-Ki
body:
Not sure if a true issue or just means the peer isnt't there, but my logs are full of lines like this : 


```
checking: (peer id, i replace it with x)
Found Matching Peer for Connection:ipv6://[::ffff:127.0.0.1]:13527
pqissllistener => Passing to pqissl module! 
(Sat Mar 21 17:22:39 2026 Z: p3peermgr, lvl: 0): p3PeerMgr::setVisState(x, 2, 0) 
(Sat Mar 21 17:23:09 2026 Z: p3peermgr, lvl: 0): p3PeerMgr::tick() removeUnusedLocations() 
(Sat Mar 21 17:23:22 2026 Z: pqisslzone, lvl: 0): pqissl::readdata() xSSL_read() SSL_ERROR_SYSCALL SOCKET_DEAD -> calling reset() errno: 0 ----WINDOWS OPERATING SYSTEM FAILURE---- 
(Sat Mar 21 17:23:22 2026 Z: pqisslzone, lvl: 0): SSL_ERROR_SYSCALL, ret == -1 errno: 0 ----WINDOWS OPERATING SYSTEM FAILURE----
 
(Sat Mar 21 17:23:22 2026 Z: pqisslzone, lvl: 0): pqissl::readdata() -> calling reset() 
(Sat Mar 21 17:26:46 2026 Z: p3peermgr, lvl: 0): pqissllistenbase::isSSLActive() Successful Peer -> Connection Okay 
(Sat Mar 21 17:26:46 2026 Z: p3peermgr, lvl: 0): pqissllistenbase::finaliseAccepts() SSL Connection Ok => finaliseConnection 
(Sat Mar 21 17:26:46 2026 Z: p3peermgr, lvl: 0): pqissllistener::finaliseConnection()
```


And then loops with same and other peer ids ( if it ain't an error, maybe change the logging to say it more explicitly? )

fatal: remote error: upload-pack: not our ref ad3cc8313df4a3c6f5b0af9e09375270e02eaee7

title: Android version?
author: Shurik1236
body:
Возможно ли появление версии для Android? Сейчас многие сидят с мобильных устройств и это позволило бы включить куда большую аудиторию.
Тем более RetroShare настолько проста, что пользоваться смогут даже домохозяйки.

Is an Android version possible? Many people use mobile devices these days, and that would allow us to reach a much larger audience.
Moreover, RetroShare is so easy to use that even housewives can use it.

title: Implement for Friend Nodes, Channels, Boards & for Wiki default Logo creation when no Avatar or Logo set....
author: defnax

title: Wiki todos V2 - implement async loading, merge, republish & comments features
author: defnax
body: Todos: 1. Remove RsGxsUpdateBroadcastPage & old Tokenque method, replace by loading data with `RsThread::async` - look for example here: [GxsChannelPostFilesModel.cpp](https://github.com/RetroShare/RetroShare/blob/master/retroshare-gui/src/gui/gxschannels/GxsChannelPostFilesModel.cpp) 2. Add if required more rsEvents for RsWiki: ``` NEW_SNAPHOT NEW_COLLECTION SUBSCRIBE_STATUS_CHANGED NEW_COMMENT ``` 3. Implement Republish & Merging function for WikiEdits (not done yet, it shows only first edited ones, for Admins/Mods) 4. Add UserNotify when you subscribed a Wiki it has new updates or pushes - Count on MainWindow/Wiki Button ( when you viewed the Wiki Page or Wiki Group clear the counts) - look example code here [GxsChannelUserNotify.cpp](https://github.com/RetroShare/RetroShare/blob/master/retroshare-gui/src/gui/gxschannels/GxsChannelUserNotify.cpp) [GxsChannelUserNotify.h](https://github.com/RetroShare/RetroShare/blob/master/retroshare-gui/src/gui/gxschannels/GxsChannelUserNotify.h) 5. Add Search Filter for the Page ( at the moment not functional) 6. Add Moderators feature, like how on forums 7. Implement the commenting feature & a CommentsViewer for the comments of a Page ( maybe as new tab on the main page of a Page) 8. Implement the features which we need for wiki for lib & gui part: ``` /* Specific Service Data */ virtual bool getCollections(const uint32_t &token, std::vector &collections) = 0; virtual bool getSnapshots(const uint32_t &token, std::vector &snapshots) = 0; virtual bool getComments(const uint32_t &token, std::vector &comments) = 0; virtual bool getRelatedSnapshots(const uint32_t &token, std::vector &snapshots) = 0; virtual bool submitCollection(uint32_t &token, RsWikiCollection &collection) = 0; virtual bool submitSnapshot(uint32_t &token, RsWikiSnapshot &snapshot) = 0; virtual bool submitComment(uint32_t &token, RsWikiComment &comment) = 0; virtual bool updateCollection(uint32_t &token, RsWikiCollection &collection) = 0; // Blocking Interfaces. virtual bool createCollection(RsWikiCollection &collection) = 0; virtual bool updateCollection(const RsWikiCollection &collection) = 0; virtual bool getCollections(const std::list groupIds, std::vector &groups) = 0; ``` > Basic Functionality of RS Wiki: > - New Group: creates a collection of Wiki Pages, the creator of group, > and anyone they share the publish keys with) is the moderator >- New Page: Create a new Wiki Page, only Group Moderators can do this, > - Edit: Anyone Can Edit the Wiki Page, and the changes form a tree under the original, > - RePublish: This is used by the Moderators to accept and reject Edits., > the republished page becomes a new version of the Root Page, allowing more edits to be done > Ideas: > - Formatting: No HTML, lets use Markdown or something similar., > - Diffs, lots of edits will lead to complex merges - a robust merge tool is essential, > - Read Mode... hide all the Edits, and only show the most recently published versions, > - Easy Duplication - to take over an Abandoned or badly moderated Wiki. Copies All base versions to a new group > - WikiLinks. A generic Wiki Cross Linking system. This should be combined with Easy Duplication option, > to allow easy replacement of groups if necessary... A good design here is critical to a successful Wiki ecosystem\, > - work out how to include media (photos, audio, video, etc) without embedding in pages, > this would leverage the turtle transfer system somehow - maybe like channels., > - Comments, reviews etc can be incorporated - ideas here are welcome., > Source path: libretroshare/service: [p3wiki.cc](https://github.com/RetroShare/libretroshare/blob/96e249a06d8f30c2aace38beecc8fb7271159a88/src/services/p3wiki.cc) [p3wiki.h](https://github.com/RetroShare/libretroshare/blob/96e249a06d8f30c2aace38beecc8fb7271159a88/src/services/p3wiki.h) rsitems: [rswikiitems.cc](https://github.com/RetroShare/libretroshare/blob/7643654403b5779e56dd20c5e73e4e47583f27e6/src/rsitems/rswikiitems.cc) [rswikiitems.h](https://github.com/RetroShare/libretroshare/blob/7643654403b5779e56dd20c5e73e4e47583f27e6/src/rsitems/rswikiitems.h ) Interface: https://github.com/RetroShare/libretroshare/blob/master/src/retroshare/rswiki.h Gui Part: https://github.com/RetroShare/RetroShare/tree/master/retroshare-gui/src/gui/WikiPoos ``` WikiAddDialog.cpp WikiAddDialog.h WikiAddDialog.ui WikiDialog.cpp WikiDialog.h WikiDialog.ui WikiEditDialog.cpp WikiEditDialog.h WikiEditDialog.ui gui/gxs/WikiGroupDialog.cpp gui/gxs/WikiGroupDialog.h ``` Enable rs Wiki: `CONFIG* = wikipoos ` ``` $ cd retroshare $ qmake $ make ``` Howto compile: Linux: [Linux_InstallGuide.md](https://github.com/RetroShare/RetroShare/blob/master/build_scripts/Debian+Ubuntu/Linux_InstallGuide.md ) Windows: [WindowsMSys2_InstallGuide.md](https://github.com/RetroShare/RetroShare/blob/master/build_scripts/Windows-msys2/WindowsMSys2_InstallGuide.md ) MacOS [MacOS_X_InstallGuide.md](https://github.com/RetroShare/RetroShare/blob/master/build_scripts/OSX/MacOS_X_InstallGuide.md) To Edit or Create Ui files you can use Qt Creator or Qt Designer

No major updates or changes to the OS between last time it worked and now not working. Note that have seen other uses reporting same issue. Appreciate this service and app. Thank you very much.

title: Wiki Todos - implement the update functionality with rsEvents
author: defnax
body:
Hello, we need help to enable RS Wiki(plugin) back.

Todo:

1. Implement rsEvents for update the Gui see example in:
[p3gxschannels.cc](https://github.com/RetroShare/libretroshare/blob/96e249a06d8f30c2aace38beecc8fb7271159a88/src/services/p3gxschannels.cc) [rsgxschannels.h](https://github.com/RetroShare/libretroshare/blob/96e249a06d8f30c2aace38beecc8fb7271159a88/src/retroshare/rsgxschannels.h) [GxsChannelDialog.cpp](https://github.com/RetroShare/RetroShare/blob/master/retroshare-gui/src/gui/gxschannels/GxsChannelDialog.cpp) [GxsChannelPostsWidgetWithModel.cpp](https://github.com/RetroShare/RetroShare/blob/master/retroshare-gui/src/gui/gxschannels/GxsChannelPostsWidgetWithModel.cpp)

2. cleanup old code and replace with new one, there are some dummycodes or non functional

Source path:
libretroshare:
`/src/services`
[p3wiki.cc](https://github.com/RetroShare/libretroshare/blob/96e249a06d8f30c2aace38beecc8fb7271159a88/src/services/p3wiki.cc)
[p3wiki.h](https://github.com/RetroShare/libretroshare/blob/96e249a06d8f30c2aace38beecc8fb7271159a88/src/services/p3wiki.h)
`/src/retroshare/rswiki.h`
https://github.com/RetroShare/libretroshare/blob/master/src/retroshare/rswiki.h

Gui Part:
https://github.com/RetroShare/RetroShare/tree/master/retroshare-gui/src/gui/WikiPoos

 WikiAddDialog.cpp
    WikiAddDialog.h
    WikiAddDialog.ui
    WikiDialog.cpp
    WikiDialog.h
    WikiDialog.ui
    WikiEditDialog.cpp
    WikiEditDialog.h
    WikiEditDialog.ui

to enable wiki for compile you need give on commandline this option
`CONFIG += wikipoos`
else add into retroshare.pri:
`CONFIG *= wikipoos`

body:
Whenever I try to start a hidden node using the Flatpak version of RS, it claims that "Tor cannot be started on your system: Cannot find tor executable. Current path is "tor"."

Tried everything I can think of to fix this, but I'm not super familiar with flatpak permissions. Any advice?
